decisions.title: Add swatch delivery option
decisions.context: Initial design had users choosing fabric directly from images. User suggested adding physical swatch samples.
decisions.decision: Add overnight swatch delivery option with multiple shipping tiers
decisions.rationale: Some users want to see/feel fabric before committing to $850 purchase. Physical samples reduce uncertainty and returns.
decisions.consequences: Created two-path flow structure. Added 4 screens to swatch path. Increased complexity but reduced purchase anxiety.

decisions.title: Create two-path flow structure
decisions.context: After adding swatches, needed to accommodate both users who want samples and those ready to order immediately.
decisions.decision: Implement two complete paths: swatch detour (10 screens) and order now (6 screens)
decisions.rationale: Respects different user preferences and confidence levels. Doesn't force committed users through unnecessary steps.
alternatives: Single path with optional swatch step
decisions.consequences: Doubled screen count. Requires session persistence. More testing surface. Better user satisfaction expected.

decisions.title: Collect choices before personal information
decisions.context: Deciding what order to collect information: product choices vs personal data.
decisions.decision: Have users choose fabric/style before requesting email, measurements, or payment info
decisions.rationale: Progressive disclosure builds trust. Lower commitment actions first. Users feel in control. Reduces abandonment.
decisions.consequences: Cannot email abandoned carts early. Slight risk of users abandoning after choices. Improved trust and completion rate expected.

decisions.title: Capture email after initial choices
decisions.context: User suggested: 'Let's try it with getting the email after she makes some choices'
decisions.decision: Request email after fabric/swatch selection but before shipping address
decisions.rationale: User has made meaningful choices, showing purchase intent. Enables abandoned cart recovery. Still early enough to feel non-invasive.
decisions.consequences: Enables session recovery. Allows abandoned cart emails. Balances conversion and marketing needs.

decisions.title: Request measurements before contact information
decisions.context: Deciding order of personal information collection.
decisions.decision: Collect body measurements before shipping address and contact details
decisions.rationale: Measurements are product-specific and feel like customization (positive). Address feels more personal/private. Build from product to personal.
decisions.consequences: Users engage with product customization before feeling transaction pressure. May increase abandonment at address step but improves quality of completions.

decisions.title: Limit swatch selection to 5
decisions.context: Needed to manage costs and prevent decision paralysis with too many swatches.
decisions.decision: Maximum 5 swatches per order with real-time counter and disabled checkboxes at limit
decisions.rationale: Manages shipping costs. Prevents decision paralysis. Creates constraint that aids decision-making. 5 is enough for comparison.
decisions.consequences: Clear expectations. Encourages thoughtful selection. May frustrate users wanting more options. Reduces operational costs.

decisions.title: Tiered swatch delivery pricing
decisions.context: User requested: 'We should have a dropdown where she can have overnight $20, 2-3 days $15, 5 days $8'
decisions.decision: Three delivery tiers: Overnight ($20), 2-3 days ($15), 5 days ($8)
decisions.rationale: User choice between speed and cost. Premium pricing for overnight offsets costs. Most users likely choose middle tier.
decisions.consequences: Added complexity to checkout. Revenue from shipping. Clear value proposition for each tier. Manages rush order expectations.

decisions.title: Offer 5% discount for swatch orders
decisions.context: Wanted to incentivize users who ordered swatches to complete their dress order.
decisions.decision: Give 5% discount on final dress order for users who ordered swatches
decisions.rationale: Rewards users who invested in swatches. Acknowledges their commitment. Encourages order completion. Creates positive surprise.
decisions.consequences: Reduces margin slightly ($42.50 on $850 order). Increases conversion likelihood. Creates loyalty. May set discount expectations.

decisions.title: Split payment from shipping screen
decisions.context: User requested: 'I think we should have a separate screen for payment methods'
decisions.decision: Create separate screen for payment methods instead of combining with shipping
decisions.rationale: Better focus on each task. Reduced cognitive load. Clearer action hierarchy. Shipping and payment are conceptually distinct.
decisions.consequences: Added one screen to flow. Improved completion rate expected. Clearer error handling. May feel longer to some users.

decisions.title: Session persistence for return flow
decisions.context: User wanted swatch path to resume where customer left off, not start over.
decisions.decision: Return flow pre-fills selections and resumes at fabric choice from received swatches
decisions.rationale: Respects user's previous work. Reduces friction. Creates seamless experience. Shows system remembers them.
decisions.consequences: Requires session management. Cookie/email-based retrieval. Better UX. Increased technical complexity.

decisions.title: Button hierarchy on swatch confirmation
decisions.context: User feedback: 'The Continue to Order Now button is too prominent'
decisions.decision: Make 'Done' primary button, demote 'Continue to order now' to small link under 'Changed your mind?'
decisions.rationale: Primary action should be completing current task (Done). Continuing to order is secondary option. Hierarchy reflects user intent priority.
decisions.consequences: Fewer users may continue to order immediately. Those who do are more intentional. Clearer completion of swatch task.

decisions.title: Consistent navigation positioning
decisions.context: User: 'The back to all scenarios is showing up in different places on each prototype screen'
decisions.decision: Position 'Back to all scenarios' link at bottom of page, outside white box, left-aligned to box edge on all screens
decisions.rationale: Consistency improves usability. Bottom position doesn't compete with primary actions. Outside box shows it's meta-navigation.
decisions.consequences: Requires scrolling to access. Clear visual hierarchy. Consistent muscle memory. Easy to find when needed.

decisions.title: Placeholder dress image with dynamic update messaging
decisions.context: Needed visual representation of dress without actual product images.
decisions.decision: Gray placeholder with text 'Dress image (updates as you choose)'
decisions.rationale: Sets expectation for dynamic updates. Placeholder doesn't mislead. Future implementation clear. Sticky positioning keeps it visible.
decisions.consequences: Doesn't show actual dress. Sets future feature expectation. Keeps layout intact for when images added. Clear communication.

decisions.title: Measurements drawer for review screen
decisions.context: Payment screen needed to show measurements for review without cluttering layout.
decisions.decision: Slide-in drawer from right with 'View' link, overlays page with backdrop
decisions.rationale: Keeps payment screen focused. Measurements available on-demand. Familiar mobile pattern. Easy to implement and use.
decisions.consequences: Requires JavaScript. May be missed by some users. Mobile-friendly pattern. Keeps focus on payment completion.

decisions.title: Radio buttons for final fabric choice, checkboxes for swatches
decisions.context: Different selection patterns needed for swatch selection vs final fabric choice.
decisions.decision: Checkboxes with 5-limit for swatches, radio buttons for single fabric choice
decisions.rationale: Checkboxes signal multiple selection. Radio buttons signal single choice. Matches user mental model and prevents errors.
alternatives: Checkboxes throughout with limit=1 for final
decisions.consequences: Clear affordances. Prevents selection errors. Familiar patterns. May need explanation for swatch limit behavior.
